# Review: `deepmind/607.lean` — Erdős Problem 607

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 607`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/607`.
- [ ] **FAIL:** Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem. The module docstring contains the title, reference link, and two bibliography entries (`[Er85]`, `[SzTr83]`) but no plain-English summary of what the problem asks. The description appears only in the theorem docstring (lines 52–59). A one-sentence summary should be added to the module docstring.
- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers are present (`[Er85]`, `[SzTr83]`).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos607 ... end Erdos607`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos607`).
- [x] No `open` / `open scoped` needed; all Mathlib names are used fully qualified (`Real.exp`, `Real.sqrt`, `Finset`, etc.).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] All four helper definitions (`Collinear607`, `lineIncidence607`, `lineSpectrum607`, `achievableSpectra607`) have LaTeX docstrings.
- [x] All definitions are marked `noncomputable` (appropriate since they involve real-number operations and `Finset` filtering over reals).
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] `Collinear607` (a `Prop`) uses `UpperCamelCase`.
- [x] `lineIncidence607`, `lineSpectrum607`, `achievableSpectra607` (value-returning functions) use `lowerCamelCase`.
- [x] No problem-specific notation needed; none introduced.
- [x] No `@[category test/API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_607` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 52–60).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er85]`, `[SzTr83]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem is a direct assertion ("There exists a constant $C > 0$ such that..."), proved by Szemerédi and Trotter. This is **Case 3** — no `answer()` wrapper needed.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (existential statement with no `answer()` wrapper). Correct for Case 3.
- [x] No misplaced quantifiers as theorem parameters; all quantification is in the theorem body.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` — valid category for a proved but not formally verified result.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: `5`, `52`).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: `5 52`.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both codes are valid AMS MSC2020 codes (`5` = combinatorics, `52` = convex and discrete geometry).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` pattern (multi-line `by\n  sorry`, which is acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches (problem is `research solved`, not `research open`).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A — no variants present. Single theorem, single file.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℝ`, `×`, `≠`, `∃`, `∀`, `≤`, `·`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest line ~80 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review.
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified during this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: 1 issue found.**

| # | Severity | Item | Detail |
|---|----------|------|--------|
| 1 | Minor | §3 Module docstring | Missing plain-English description of the problem. Add a brief summary (e.g., "Erdős asked for an upper bound on the number of distinct line spectra achievable by $n$-point configurations in $\mathbb{R}^2$; Szemerédi and Trotter proved it is at most $\exp(C\sqrt{n})$.") to the module docstring. |

The formalization is otherwise clean: definitions are well-documented with correct casing and `noncomputable` annotations, the theorem uses the correct Case 3 pattern (direct assertion, no `answer()`), attributes are properly formatted, and code style is consistent.

    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 607
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/607](https://www.erdosproblems.com/607)
    23→
    24→[Er85] Erdős, P., *Problems and results in combinatorial geometry*.
    25→
    26→[SzTr83] Szemerédi, E. and Trotter, W. T., *Extremal problems in discrete geometry* (1983).
    27→-/
    28→
    29→namespace Erdos607
    30→
    31→/-- Three points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ are collinear: the cross product of the displacement
    32→    vectors $(q - p)$ and $(r - p)$ vanishes. -/
    33→noncomputable def Collinear607 (p q r : ℝ × ℝ) : Prop :=
    34→  (q.1 - p.1) * (r.2 - p.2) = (r.1 - p.1) * (q.2 - p.2)
    35→
    36→/-- Given a finite point set $P$ and two points $p, q$, the number of points in $P$
    37→    lying on the line through $p$ and $q$. -/
    38→noncomputable def lineIncidence607 (P : Finset (ℝ × ℝ)) (p q : ℝ × ℝ) : ℕ :=
    39→  (P.filter (fun r => Collinear607 p q r)).card
    40→
    41→/-- The line spectrum of a finite point set $P \subset \mathbb{R}^2$: the set of all distinct
    42→    values $|\ell \cap P|$ as $\ell$ ranges over lines determined by $P$ (lines through at
    43→    least two points of $P$). -/
    44→noncomputable def lineSpectrum607 (P : Finset (ℝ × ℝ)) : Finset ℕ :=
    45→  P.biUnion fun p =>
    46→    (P.filter fun q => p ≠ q).image fun q => lineIncidence607 P p q
    47→
    48→/-- The set of achievable line spectra for $n$-point configurations in $\mathbb{R}^2$. -/
    49→noncomputable def achievableSpectra607 (n : ℕ) : Set (Finset ℕ) :=
    50→  {S | ∃ P : Finset (ℝ × ℝ), P.card = n ∧ lineSpectrum607 P = S}
    51→
    52→/--
    53→**Erdős Problem 607** [Er85]:
    54→
    55→There exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$, the number
    56→$F(n)$ of distinct line spectra achievable by $n$-point configurations in $\mathbb{R}^2$
    57→satisfies $F(n) \leq \exp(C \cdot \sqrt{n})$.
    58→
    59→Proved by Szemerédi and Trotter [SzTr83].
    60→-/
    61→@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
    62→theorem erdos_607 :
    63→    ∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧ ∃ n₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, n₀ ≤ n →
    64→      (achievableSpectra607 n).Finite ∧
    65→      ((achievableSpectra607 n).ncard : ℝ) ≤ Real.exp (C * Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) := by
    66→  sorry
    67→
    68→end Erdos607
    69→

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 607

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/607](https://www.erdosproblems.com/607)

[Er85] Erdős, P., *Problems and results in combinatorial geometry*.

[SzTr83] Szemerédi, E. and Trotter, W. T., *Extremal problems in discrete geometry* (1983).
-/

namespace Erdos607

/-- Three points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ are collinear: the cross product of the displacement
    vectors $(q - p)$ and $(r - p)$ vanishes. -/
noncomputable def Collinear607 (p q r : ℝ × ℝ) : Prop :=
  (q.1 - p.1) * (r.2 - p.2) = (r.1 - p.1) * (q.2 - p.2)

/-- Given a finite point set $P$ and two points $p, q$, the number of points in $P$
    lying on the line through $p$ and $q$. -/
noncomputable def lineIncidence607 (P : Finset (ℝ × ℝ)) (p q : ℝ × ℝ) : ℕ :=
  (P.filter (fun r => Collinear607 p q r)).card

/-- The line spectrum of a finite point set $P \subset \mathbb{R}^2$: the set of all distinct
    values $|\ell \cap P|$ as $\ell$ ranges over lines determined by $P$ (lines through at
    least two points of $P$). -/
noncomputable def lineSpectrum607 (P : Finset (ℝ × ℝ)) : Finset ℕ :=
  P.biUnion fun p =>
    (P.filter fun q => p ≠ q).image fun q => lineIncidence607 P p q

/-- The set of achievable line spectra for $n$-point configurations in $\mathbb{R}^2$. -/
noncomputable def achievableSpectra607 (n : ℕ) : Set (Finset ℕ) :=
  {S | ∃ P : Finset (ℝ × ℝ), P.card = n ∧ lineSpectrum607 P = S}

/--
**Erdős Problem 607** [Er85]:

There exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$, the number
$F(n)$ of distinct line spectra achievable by $n$-point configurations in $\mathbb{R}^2$
satisfies $F(n) \leq \exp(C \cdot \sqrt{n})$.

Proved by Szemerédi and Trotter [SzTr83].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
theorem erdos_607 :
    ∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧ ∃ n₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, n₀ ≤ n →
      (achievableSpectra607 n).Finite ∧
      ((achievableSpectra607 n).ncard : ℝ) ≤ Real.exp (C * Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos607

content.text: The review found 1 minor issue: the module docstring is missing a plain-English description of the problem. Let me add one.

input.new_string:
*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/607](https://www.erdosproblems.com/607)

Erdős asked for an upper bound on the number of distinct line spectra achievable by
$n$-point configurations in $\mathbb{R}^2$. Szemerédi and Trotter proved it is at most
$\exp(C\sqrt{n})$ for some constant $C > 0$.

[Er85]

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 607

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/607](https://www.erdosproblems.com/607)

[Er85] Erdős, P., *Problems and results in combinatorial geometry*.

[SzTr83] Szemerédi, E. and Trotter, W. T., *Extremal problems in discrete geometry* (1983).
-/

namespace Erdos607

/-- Three points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ are collinear: the cross product of the displacement
    vectors $(q - p)$ and $(r - p)$ vanishes. -/
noncomputable def Collinear607 (p q r : ℝ × ℝ) : Prop :=
  (q.1 - p.1) * (r.2 - p.2) = (r.1 - p.1) * (q.2 - p.2)

/-- Given a finite point set $P$ and two points $p, q$, the number of points in $P$

noncomputable def lineIncidence607 (P : Finset (ℝ × ℝ)) (p q : ℝ × ℝ) : ℕ :=

/-- The line spectrum of a finite point set $P \subset \mathbb{R}^2$: the set of all distinct

noncomputable def lineSpectrum607 (P : Finset (ℝ × ℝ)) : Finset ℕ :=

noncomputable def achievableSpectra607 (n : ℕ) : Set (Finset ℕ) :=

/--

Proved by Szemerédi and Trotter [SzTr83].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
theorem erdos_607 :
    ∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧ ∃ n₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, n₀ ≤ n →
      (achievableSpectra607 n).Finite ∧
      ((achievableSpectra607 n).ncard : ℝ) ≤ Real.exp (C * Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos607
